page: Amazon Web Services r6in.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and includes a 54 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the r6in.4xlarge in the average performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is recorded at 1892.38, while the multi-core score reaches 15467.9. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 829.62 GB/sec and a large block uncached write speed of 56.42 GB/sec. In database operations, the instance achieves 15.9 million Redis SET operations per second and a Passmark database score of 7776.01. For language model tasks, text generation using a small 135M model is a strong point at 272.59 tokens per second, though prompt processing for a medium 7B model drops to a weaker 31.94 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6in.4xlarge offers a high-density memory configuration relative to its compute resources, making it a viable choice for memory-bound applications that do not require local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is the complete reliance on network-attached EBS storage, which may introduce latency compared to local SSDs, though this is mitigated by the 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. This instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as relational and non-relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data buffering, and medium-scale enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r6in.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, establishing a high 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external connectivity. Performance benchmarks indicate solid mid-tier capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 21082.67 and a Redis SET performance of 15.9 million operations per second. It also demonstrates strong text generation performance on small language models at 272.59 tokens per second. Lacking local NVMe drives, the r6in.4xlarge is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy applications that rely on network-attached storage, making it well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for in-memory databases and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-bandwidth networking for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor at 3.5 GHz
128.0 GB of DDR4 memory optimized for memory-bound applications
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor optimization
Delivers 15.9 million Redis SET operations per second in benchmark testing
No local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
